events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, Brian May, was born on July 19, 1947, in Hampton, Middlesex, England.

angles.name: Guitar Legend
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the iconic Brian May! His guitar work with Queen is simply out of this world. What's your favourite May solo?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones' flamboyant frontman, Mick Jagger, was born on July 26, 1943, in Dartford, Kent, England.

angles.name: Rock's Eternal Youth
angles.copy: Still got the moves like Jagger! The one and only Mick Jagger celebrates his 83rd birthday today. What's your favourite Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: MTV, the groundbreaking music television channel, launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its very first music video.

angles.name: MTV's First Moment
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched and changed music forever! Kicking off with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' – how ironic is that for radio? What was the first music video you remember seeing?

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis's iconic debut album, 'Definitely Maybe', was released on August 29, 1994, becoming the fastest-selling debut album in British history at the time.

angles.name: Britpop Debut
angles.copy: It's 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed the sound of British music. What's your favourite track from this absolute classic?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Three Days of Peace
angles.copy: 57 years ago today, hundreds of thousands gathered for Woodstock – three days of peace, love, and music that defined a generation. Imagine being there!

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's Death (1977)
events.blurb: Elvis Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977, leaving an indelible mark on music and culture.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King of Rock & Roll, Elvis Presley, who left us 49 years ago today. His music continues to inspire generations. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's Death (1970)
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, widely regarded as one of the greatest guitarists of all time, died on September 18, 1970.

angles.name: Guitar God Tribute
angles.copy: Remembering the legendary Jimi Hendrix, who passed away 56 years ago today. His innovation on the guitar redefined music forever. What's your favourite Hendrix riff?

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles' eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', featuring its iconic zebra crossing cover, was released on September 26, 1969 in the UK and October 1, 1969 in the US.

angles.name: Iconic Album Cover
angles.copy: 57 years ago, The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road' and one of the most famous album covers ever. Which track is on repeat for you?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, lead singer and founder of The Pretenders, was born on September 7, 1951, in Akron, Ohio, USA.

angles.name: Pretenders Frontwoman
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! The voice of The Pretenders, still rocking after all these years. What's your favourite Pretenders hit?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Oasis and Beady Eye frontman, Liam Gallagher, was born on September 21, 1972, in Manchester, England.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that undeniable swagger to the stage. What's your favourite Liam moment?

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the US on October 11, 1971, and as part of the album 'Imagine' on September 9, 1971 in the US and October 8, 1971 in the UK.

angles.name: A Song for Peace
angles.copy: 55 years since John Lennon released 'Imagine', a timeless call for peace and unity. What does this song mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising how people listened to music.

angles.name: Music in Your Pocket
angles.copy: Remember the first iPod? 25 years ago, Apple changed the game, putting '1,000 songs in your pocket'. What was the first album you loaded onto yours?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the release date of the very first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! From iconic themes to unforgettable gadgets, which Bond film or song is your all-time favourite? Licence to thrill!

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, co-founder of The Beatles and an iconic solo artist, was born on October 9, 1940, in Liverpool, England.

angles.name: Remembering a Legend
angles.copy: Remembering John Lennon, who would have been 86 today. His music and message continue to resonate globally. What's the Lennon song that moves you most?

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: The legendary British actor, comedian, and writer, John Cleese, known for Monty Python and Fawlty Towers, was born on October 27, 1939, in Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, England.

angles.name: Comedy Icon
angles.copy: Happy 87th birthday to the utterly brilliant John Cleese! From Python to Fawlty Towers, his genius is unmatched. What's your favourite Cleese moment?

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album' (1968)
events.blurb: The Beatles' self-titled double album, commonly known as 'The White Album', was released on November 22, 1968.

angles.name: The White Album Anniversary
angles.copy: It's 58 years since The Beatles dropped 'The White Album' – a sprawling, diverse masterpiece. Which track from this double LP is your go-to?
